summary: Matched the Home-Run Bat item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itbat.s with C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itbat.c, switching itbat.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, and updating obj_files.mk to build the C object. The PR also expanded itbat.h prototypes, moved the bat spawn prototype out of the generic it_27CF headers, added a small itBatAttributes struct in itCommonItems.h, and cleaned includes in it_26B1.c. No human comments or review feedback were present in the slice.

key_files.role: new matched C implementation for bat item behavior and state table
expanded public prototypes for itbat symbols
removed it_80284854 declaration from generic item header
removed stale documentation declaration for it_80284854
include cleanup to use itbat.h and corrected itsword include path
switched build object from asm itbat.s.o to src itbat.c.o

what_changed: Deleted 445 lines of handwritten/disassembled assembly from asm/melee/it/items/itbat.s.
Added a 212-line C implementation for the bat item in src/melee/it/items/itbat.c.

Recreated the it_803F57B0 ItemStateTable as a C array with five state entries using callbacks such as it_802849B8, it_802849C0, it_802849C4, it_80284A2C, it_80284A34, it_80284A80, it_80284C38, it_80284C40, and it_80284C44.

Implemented it_80284854 as the bat spawner using SpawnItem, It_Kind_Bat, zero velocity initialization, facing_dir = -1.0F, x44_flag.bits.b0 = true, Item_80268B5C, and post-spawn initialization via it_80284974.

Implemented bat item state transitions and callbacks including it_80284974, it_802849F0, it_80284AAC, it_80284B30, it_80284BA0, it_80284C10, and collision callbacks around itColl_BounceOffVictim and itColl_BounceOffShield.
Added itBatAttributes to itCommonItems.h with fields x0, x4, x8, and xC_vel; it_80284910 uses xC_vel from item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to initialize vertical velocity.
Expanded itbat.h from a partial list to declarations for all newly implemented itbat symbols and the extern ItemStateTable.
Removed it_80284854 from it_27CF.h and it_27CF.dox, relocating ownership of the bat spawn function to itbat.h.
Updated configure.py to mark Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itbat.c") instead of NonMatching.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itbat.c.o instead of the assembly object.
smart_moves: Kept the object-level matching workflow complete: delete asm, add C, mark the object Matching in configure.py, and update obj_files.mk in the same PR.
Modeled the old data section as a typed ItemStateTable array rather than leaving raw .4byte state-table entries, making callback relationships searchable and maintainable.
Used existing item helper macros and APIs such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, Item_80268B5C, and Item_80268E5C instead of preserving raw struct offsets everywhere.
Named the special attribute field xC_vel, capturing useful semantic information from the 0xC attribute load used in it_80284910 while leaving unknown earlier fields as x0, x4, and x8.
Moved the bat spawn prototype out of the broad it_27CF headers into itbat.h, improving symbol locality for future item-module work.
decomp_lessons: For item modules, a successful match usually requires synchronized source, headers, build-object lists, and configure matching status; missing any one of these leaves the project building stale asm or treating matched C as nonmatching.
Item state tables can be reconstructed cleanly as ItemStateTable arrays once callback prototypes are available; this is preferable to keeping anonymous data-section words.

SpawnItem initialization order and field choices matter for matching: this PR preserves pos->z = 0.0F, prev_pos/pos copies, facing_dir = -1.0F, damage = 0, zero velocity, null parent gobj fields, and x44_flag.bits.b0 = true before calling Item_80268B5C.
When an item callback only returns false or does nothing, matching C can use simple bool-returning stubs and void-returning empty callbacks, matching common item-state callback patterns.
Special-attribute structs can be introduced incrementally: only name the field with understood semantics, as with itBatAttributes.xC_vel, and keep unknown fields offset-style.
Header cleanup should follow ownership: item-specific symbols such as it_80284854 belong in itbat.h rather than a broad it_27CF declaration header.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based symbol names such as it_80284854, it_80284910, it_80284974, it_802849F0, it_80284AAC, and it_80284D34 because no richer names were evidenced.
Used item module header naming src/melee/it/items/itbat.h for bat-specific prototypes.
Used typedef struct itBatAttributes with offset-style unknown fields x0, x4, x8 and a partially semantic field xC_vel.
Kept the data symbol name it_803F57B0 for the bat ItemStateTable.
Used common item variable names seen in the C implementation: gobj, item, attr, ap, child, pos, and spawn.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted sdata2 constants 0.0 and -1.0 into C float literals 0.0F and -1.0F in spawn and velocity setup.
Matched the assembly state-table .4byte layout by using five ItemStateTable initializer rows with -1 or 0 animation IDs and the corresponding callback functions.
Matched raw child-JObj access patterns by using HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)) or HSD_JObjGetChild(gobj->hsd_obj), then clearing JOBJ_HIDDEN with HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll.
Matched landing/state reset behavior by writing item->xD50_landNum = 0 in the same initialization/state-transition routines that call Item_80268E5C.
Matched fall physics logic by using ItemAttr fields x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max with it_80272860, followed by it_80274658(gobj, it_804D6D28->x68_float).

Matched collision behavior with small wrappers: conditional itColl_BounceOffVictim when item->msid == 3, unconditional victim bounce callbacks, shield bounce via itColl_BounceOffShield, and forwarding it_80284CAC to it_80273030.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename it_80284854 or other itbat symbols to semantic bat function names.
Search for additional item modules that define special attribute structs in itCommonItems.h using offset-style unknown fields plus one semantic field.
Compare itbat state-transition wrappers against itsword or itstar for reusable item matching patterns around Item_80268E5C and ItemStateTable.
Check whether Item_GObj and HSD_GObj prototype usage for it_80284D34 was later standardized across item headers.
